title: AEP v1.2 Bug Ontology Record
description: v1.2 immune-system primitive (operator source.md L15-18). Every bug becomes a typed memory object. Not just 'fixed bug' but: bug class / root cause / escape path / detection gap / affected primitive / reproduction input / smallest failing example / prevention rule / regression test / future warning cue. Operator framing: 'A normal software repo has tests. AEP should have scar tissue.' Lineage EXTENDS structured bug taxonomies (CWE/CVE) + post-mortem culture (Google SRE) + structured fault analysis. Composes_with: v1.2 F20 BugVaccineKernel (each bug ontology record births a vaccine) + v1.1 F13 ClaimRuntimeFalsifier + v1.1 F16 AttackClass registry.

type.description: Discriminator. Always 'BugOntologyRecord'. New claim type added to data/claims.jsonl per operator L15.

bug_class.description: Operator L15 verbatim. Bug class taxonomy. Extensible via v1.2.1+ amendment per schema-additive-only discipline.

root_cause.description: Operator L15 verbatim field 2. The underlying cause that made the bug possible.

escape_path.description: Operator L15 verbatim field 3. How the bug escaped from authoring to production / promotion.

detection_gap.description: Operator L15 verbatim field 4. Which validator / gate / review SHOULD have caught this but did not, and why.

affected_primitive.description: Operator L15 verbatim field 5. Which AEP primitive(s) this bug affects.

input_text_or_path.description: Operator L15 verbatim field 6. The exact input that triggers the bug.

example_text_or_path.description: Operator L15 verbatim field 7. Hand-reduced smallest failing example. Often a single-claim packet that triggers the bug.

executable_form.description: Operator L15 verbatim field 8. The rule that prevents recurrence. Expressed in policy-as-code form (Rego) or jsonpath or schema constraint.

test_path.description: Operator L15 verbatim field 9. Path to the regression test.

test_outcome_pre_fix.description: Before the fix, the test MUST fail (otherwise the test does not actually test the bug).

test_outcome_post_fix.description: After the fix, the test MUST pass.

future_warning_cue.description: Operator L15 verbatim field 10. The cue that surfaces on a future packet resembling this bug class. Plain-language civilian text.

external_precedents.description: F18 lineage. Bug Ontology EXTENDS: CWE (Common Weakness Enumeration) + CVE (Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures) + Google SRE post-mortem culture + structured fault tree analysis.

examples.root_cause: VG04 rubric language did not distinguish load-bearing vs decorative list items in failure_prevented[]; readers applied different mental models scoring decorative items as either 0 (irrelevant) or 5 (present).
examples.escape_path: Rubric authored alongside SPEC v1.0.3; not stress-tested with multiple independent readers before VG04 went live. the agent mean 4.00 vs warden mean 3.00 surfaced the gap.
examples.detection_gap: v1.1 F14 RaterQuorumAttestation enforces principal independence but does NOT enforce rubric-language completeness on list-valued recall fields. F14 caught the inter-rater divergence (its job) but the rubric design upstream had the bug.

reproduction_input.input_text_or_path: VG04 blind-recall on adversary AEP using cue 'premortem weakest-assumption' produced delta 1.00 across 3 attempts.

smallest_failing_example.example_text_or_path: Hand-reduced: a single rubric scoring a 3-item failure_prevented[] list where 2 items are load-bearing and 1 is decorative; two independent readers disagree on whether the decorative item counts.

prevention_rule.executable_form: policy.deny[reason] { input.rubric.evaluated_field_type == 'array'; not input.rubric.rubric_field_class_per_item; reason := 'array-valued rubric must class each item as load_bearing | decorative | evidence_anchor' }

examples.future_warning_cue: This packet's rubric does not separate critical findings from supporting details — reviewers may score it inconsistently.
